channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - gameinformer.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for gameinformer.com

item.title: Assassin's Creed Creator Reveals Witchcraft Action Game 1666: Amsterdam
item.content:encoded:
Patrice Désilets and Panache Studios have revealed their second game, 1666: Amsterdam. This new action game lets players control a witch in the titular city.

Players control Noa Brooklyn, who is living in Amsterdam, which is inhabited by entities called The Originals. These long-living creatures have accumulated and abused their power, but it's up to Noa to take that power back due to her role as a hunter of sorts called a Collector. 

To do that, Noa uses magic to expose and destroy the demonic Originals in an open-world Amsterdam. The Originals resemble normal humans, so it takes a keen eye and plenty of magical tricks to locate them. Noa will have help from 300 years in the future in the form of Aaron, a person hailing from 1999 who is transported back in time and occupies the body of a black cat. And yes, it looks like you can control Aaron in his feline form. 

Désilets took the Summer Game Fest stage to reveal that the game is launching into Early Access later this year, and that players can download a free playable prologue today on Steam and the Epic Games Store. 



https://gameinformer.com/sgf-2026/2026/06/05/assassins-creed-creator-reveals-witchcraft-action-game-1666-amsterdam
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Star Wars Zero Company, The Single-Player Tactics Game, Gets August Release Date And Anakin Skywalker In New Trailer
item.content:encoded:
Star Wars Zero Company, the single-player turn-based tactics game coming from Star Wars Jedi: Survivor developer Respawn Entertainment and Bit Reactor, launches this August. More specifically, it will launch on August 27 on PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X/S, and PC, narrowly avoiding the September onslaught of games, which includes Game Informer cover story game The Blood of Dawnwalker, Marvel's Wolverine, Control: Resonant, and more.

This release date was revealed in a new gameplay trailer that aired during the Summer Game Fest livestream today, giving us our best (and first real) look at the latest Star Wars game yet. It also gave us our first look at Anakin Skywalker in the game. Considering Bit Reactor, which is a studio formed by industry veterans who worked on XCOM and more, is one of the primary developers behind it, it's no surprise that it looks like an exciting tactics adventure in the beloved science-fantasy universe. 

Check it out for yourself below in the Star Wars Zero Company release date trailer: 

In Star Wars Zero Company, players control Hawks, a former Republic officer, who commands a ragtag group of mercenaries and bounty hunters from across the Clone Wars-era galaxy to combat a dangerous new threat. Hawks' class and appearance are fully customizable, and the teammates you recruit will come in the form of classic Star Wars characters, including Clone Troopers, astromechs, Jedi, and more. And yes, the teammates can be customized, too. 

Player choice will shape both the narrative and gameplay, as you increase bonds between squadmates to unlock new abilities and powerful synergies. Outside of combat, you can build a base and send out intelligence informants to learn your enemy's plans and more. 

Again, Star Wars Zero Company hits PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X/S, and PC on August 27, 2026. 

In the meantime, check out these screenshots from Star Wars Celebration Japan, and then check out the original Star Wars Zero Company reveal trailer. 

Are you picking up Star Wars Zero Company on day one? Let us know why in the comments below!



https://gameinformer.com/sgf-2026/2026/06/05/star-wars-zero-company-the-single-player-tactics-game-gets-august-release-date
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Guild Wars 3 Announced For PC And PS5 With A Beta Coming Next Year
item.content:encoded:
Guild Wars 2 came out nearly 14 years ago, and after a decade and a half of support and expansions, the team is pushing out a proper sequel. Guild Wars 3 was announced at Summer Game Fest this evening, and developer ArenaNet is calling it the modern evolution of the MMORPG. Check out the reveal trailer below:

"Explore Tyria like never before in Guild Wars 3, an action-adventure MMORPG and modern evolution of the genre," the trailer's description reads. "Play solo or form a band of adventurers, forging impactful and enduring bonds with fellow players, a formative cast of memorable NPCs, and the nature spirits that call Orr home."

While the teaser doesn't show us any concrete gameplay, it does show off a stunning, vibrant art style, which we hope will translate over to the full release. The announcement also came with the news that the game will be coming to both PC and PS5, which is a significant move for a series that has typically avoided console releases.

You'll be able to get your hands on the game in Fall 2027, when the beta begins. For more information, the trailer points viewers towards guildwars3.com. It's also available to be wishlisted on Steam.

For more coverage of Summer Game Fest 2026, head to gameinformer.com/sgf



https://gameinformer.com/sgf-2026/2026/06/05/guild-wars-3-announced-for-pc-and-ps5-with-a-beta-coming-next-year
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Star Wars: Galactic Racer Fully Commits To Pod Racing In Its SGF Trailer
item.content:encoded:
To date, Star Wars: Galactic Racer has only teased pod racing, but now it is officially podracing. The Summer Game Fest trailer introduces the planet Domenikos shows plenty of podracing, and confirms that Sebulba will be in the game.

The trailer also teased all kinds of new characters and lots of story content, though beyond attempting to become a legendary racer, it's not clear exactly what  the story is about.

We also finally learned the release date: October 6.



https://gameinformer.com/sgf-2026/2026/06/05/star-wars-galactic-racer-fully-commits-to-pod-racing-in-its-sgf-trailer
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
